mechanism_narrative: Persistent high inflation erodes purchasing power and corporate margins, leading to lower earnings expectations and thus negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: If CPIAUCSL consistently rises but S&P 500 forward 20-day returns are positive more than 55% of the time, the hypothesis is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: A significant increase in the unemployment rate signals a weakening labor market, which translates into reduced consumer spending and corporate profits, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: If UNRATE rises by more than 0.5 percentage points over 3 months, but S&P 500 forward 60-day returns are positive in over 50% of such instances.

mechanism_narrative: High retail sales growth combined with a stable federal funds rate indicates robust consumer demand without immediate monetary tightening pressure, supporting strong corporate revenues and positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS growth > 0.5% MoM and FEDFUNDS change < 0.1% MoM
failure_condition: If periods of high RSAFS growth and stable FEDFUNDS do not lead to positive S&P 500 20-day returns in at least 65% of occurrences.

mechanism_narrative: During periods of aggressive monetary tightening, rising federal funds rates increase borrowing costs for corporations and consumers, dampening economic activity and leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: If during identified monetary tightening cycles, the S&P 500 60-day forward returns are positive more often than negative, or the Rank IC is consistently below 0.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in housing starts indicates strong investment and consumer confidence in the economy, which typically precedes broader economic expansion and positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: If HOUST shows consistent growth but S&P 500 forward 60-day returns are negative in more than 45% of observations over a 5-year period.

mechanism_narrative: A significant decline in industrial production signals a contraction in manufacturing and overall supply-side activity, indicating weakening economic fundamentals and leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: If INDPRO declines by more than 1.0% month-over-month, but S&P 500 forward 20-day returns are positive in over 50% of such instances.

mechanism_narrative: Strong employment growth (PAYEMS) coupled with robust retail sales (RSAFS) indicates a healthy consumer base with disposable income, driving corporate earnings and leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS growth > 0.1% MoM and RSAFS growth > 0.2% MoM
failure_condition: If periods of simultaneous strong PAYEMS and RSAFS growth do not result in positive S&P 500 5-day returns in at least 70% of cases.

mechanism_narrative: During periods of economic recession, a sharp decline in GDP signals broad economic contraction, leading to significant reductions in corporate profits and thus negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 declines for two consecutive quarters
failure_condition: If GDPC1 declines for two consecutive quarters, but the S&P 500 60-day forward returns are positive in more than 40% of such periods.
